_meta.author: Claude (scorecard-refine-local autopilot)
_meta.note: Atlanta City Council batch 2 (Districts 1, 2, 4, 5 + At-Large Post 3) — evidence-based local finalization. Roster verified current June 2026: Winston and Bakhtiari re-elected unopposed Nov 4, 2025; Dozier re-elected 68.96%; Collins (sworn in Dec 2024 via special runoff) re-elected unopposed; Bond won the open District 2 seat 64%, took office Jan 5, 2026. Scores reset to null where no evidence; FALSE only on documented votes/sponsorships/statements/endorsements; TRUE only on affirmative documented alignment (public_justice q0 high bar: advocacy, not routine budget votes). Individual votes NOT inferred from 'unanimous' reporting where no per-member roll call was published (Collins/Winston on the April 2026 ICE resolutions → null).

profile.background: Atlanta native; civil-rights attorney. B.A. psychology (Spelman), M.S. (Georgia State), J.D. cum laude (NCCU Law); Director, Equity Assistance Center-South, Southern Education Foundation. APS Board of Education District 6 member 2013-2024 (chair/vice-chair). Won the Dec 3, 2024 special runoff for At-Large Post 3 (60.0% over Nicole Evans Jones); sworn in Dec 16, 2024; re-elected unopposed Nov 4, 2025 (89,913 votes), term to Jan 2030.

eshe-collins.notes_set: Atlanta City Council At-Large Post 3; sworn in Dec 16, 2024 (special runoff); re-elected unopposed Nov 4, 2025. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] NOT on the council 2020-2023, so the 2022 abortion resolutions and the 2021/2023 training-center votes are not hers. HARD evidence: 2024 Capital B questionnaire — supports continuing the Public Safety Training Center ('Cop City': Yes), a public stance against the defund/stop-cop-city movement — public_justice q0 TRUE (also supported putting the referendum on the ballot and dropping the city's appeal). 2024 Center for Civic Innovation questionnaire: city should allocate more resources to shelters/housing/healthcare 'specifically designed for transgender individuals' and form 'a dedicated board of transgender community members to advise the City Council' — biblical_marriage q2/q4 FALSE; Georgia Equality endorsed her 2017 school-board race. On the APS board she voted YES in the 5-4 KIPP charter renewal/lease vote (2018) — single renewal vote, insufficient for the 'universal school choice' cell, left null. As APS board chair joined the unanimous resolution opposing Buckhead cityhood — noted, not scored as a state-mandate cell (consistent with Wan). April 2026 ICE resolutions passed 'unanimously' but no per-member roll call published — border cell null, not inferred. Frames gun violence as a public-health crisis (Cure Violence) — noted, not scored. No religion/church publicly identified. NO EVIDENCE on abortion, marriage definition, parental rights, Christian liberty, elections, bail, or 2A — null, not penalized.

2.note: 2024 questionnaire (her own answers): city should allocate more resources to shelters, housing, and healthcare 'specifically designed for transgender individuals' — advocates trans-affirming policy, contrary to affirming biological sex as immutable.

4.note: Proposed 'a dedicated board of transgender community members to advise the City Council'; Georgia Equality endorsed her 2017 school-board race — promotes LGBTQ+ identity in public policy rather than opposing it.

0.note: 2024 Capital B questionnaire: supports continuing the $109M Public Safety Training Center ('Yes') — an affirmative public stance backing police training infrastructure against the defund/stop-cop-city push (both runoff candidates backed it, per Capital B).

profile.background: Army officer 2006-2012 (recon, Iraq/Afghanistan; Bronze Star); Denison University B.A., UGA MPA, Georgia State M.S. Real Estate; Hire Heroes USA. Unseated incumbent Cleta Winslow with ~62% in the Nov 2021 runoff; District 4 member since Jan 2022; re-elected Nov 4, 2025 (68.96%) over DeBorah Williams. Council's leading housing-abundance/YIMBY advocate.

jason-dozier.notes_set: Atlanta City Council District 4; re-elected Nov 4, 2025, 3,781-1,702 (68.96%), term to Jan 2030. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] HARD evidence: named co-sponsor of post-Dobbs 22-R-3711 (no city funds to record/investigate abortion reports; APD lowest priority) and of the May 2022 pre-Dobbs abortion-rights resolution; voted with the unanimous council for 22-R-3981 ($300K to the National Network of Abortion Funds) — sanctity_of_life FALSE. Georgia Equality endorsement (2021), accepted with a pledge to be 'an ally and advocate in the march towards equality and justice' for LGBTQ Atlantans — biblical_marriage q0 FALSE. One of 4 NO votes on the June 2023 Public Safety Training Center funding (11-4); 2021: 'Public safety shouldn't be centered around policing... we must invest in wraparound services' — public_justice q0 FALSE. Named co-sponsor of BOTH April 2026 ICE resolutions (26-R-3361 opposing ICE detention facilities; 26-R-3363 APD documentation of federal immigration enforcement) — border q2 FALSE. Opposed Buckhead cityhood (tax-base grounds) — noted, not scored as a state-mandate cell. Atlanta DSA recommended him 2025. No religion/church stated. NO EVIDENCE on schools/parental rights, Christian liberty, fiscal restraint, elections, bail, or 2A — null, not penalized.

0.note: Named co-sponsor of post-Dobbs 22-R-3711 (no city funds to record/investigate abortion reports; APD lowest priority) and of the May 2022 pre-Dobbs abortion-rights/'reproductive justice' resolution.

1.note: Voted with the unanimous council for 22-R-3981 donating up to $300,000 of city funds to the National Network of Abortion Funds — advocacy contrary to abolition.

0.note: Georgia Equality (LGBTQ-rights) endorsement, accepted with a pledge to be 'an ally and advocate in the march towards equality and justice' for LGBTQ Atlantans — does not affirm marriage as exclusively one man and one woman.

One of 4 NO votes on the June 2023 Public Safety Training Center funding (11-4); 2021: 'Public safety shouldn't be centered around policing... we must invest in wraparound services centered on social work, community organizing, and economic development.'

2.note: Named co-sponsor of both April 2026 resolutions: 26-R-3361 (opposing ICE detention facilities in Atlanta) and 26-R-3363 (requiring APD documentation/transparency on federal immigration enforcement).

profile.background: Georgia State University business degree (finance/marketing) and marketing master's; small-business owner and GSU instructor; Grant Park resident. District 1 member since Jan 2022; re-elected unopposed Nov 4, 2025 (8,071 votes). Chaired City Utilities (2023) and Community Development/Human Services (2024-25); chairs Finance/Executive (2026).

jason-winston.notes_set: Atlanta City Council District 1; re-elected unopposed Nov 4, 2025, term to Jan 2030. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] HARD evidence: named co-sponsor of post-Dobbs 22-R-3711 (no city funds to record/investigate abortion reports; APD lowest priority) and co-sponsor of the May 2022 pre-Dobbs abortion-rights resolution; voted with the unanimous council for 22-R-3981 ($300K to the National Network of Abortion Funds) — sanctity_of_life FALSE. Co-sponsored Nov 2024 legislation funding a $150K feasibility study for Atlanta's first city-backed LGBTQ Community Center; attended the trans-flag rainbow-crosswalk ribbon-cutting — biblical_marriage q4 FALSE (no marriage/trans statements in his own words found — q0/q2 null). Voted YES on the June 2023 Public Safety Training Center funding (11-4) with affirmative public advocacy: 'I will not allow Atlanta to become a place where our families live in fear of crime and violence' — public_justice q0 TRUE (abstained at one committee stage before the YES). NOT a named sponsor of the April 2026 ICE resolutions; passage reported 'unanimous' but no per-member roll call published — border cell null, not inferred. No religion/church stated. NO EVIDENCE on schools/parental rights, Christian liberty, fiscal-restraint dissents, elections, bail, home rule, or 2A — null, not penalized.

0.note: Named co-sponsor of post-Dobbs 22-R-3711 (no city funds to record/investigate abortion reports; APD lowest priority) and of the May 2022 pre-Dobbs abortion-rights resolution.

1.note: Voted with the unanimous council for 22-R-3981 ($300K of city funds to the National Network of Abortion Funds); post-Dobbs he lamented the ruling's effect on 'every woman who lacks the resources for a safe abortion' (per AJC).

4.note: Co-sponsored Nov 2024 legislation funding a $150K feasibility study for Atlanta's first city-backed LGBTQ Community Center; attended the trans-flag rainbow-crosswalk ribbon-cutting — city-policy promotion of LGBTQ+ identity, not opposition.

0.note: Voted YES on the June 2023 $67M Public Safety Training Center funding (11-4) with affirmative public advocacy: 'I made a promise to every single one of them: I will not allow Atlanta to become a place where our families live in fear of crime and violence.'

profile.background: Labor organizer (Starbucks Workers United, United Campus Workers of Georgia) and former education-policy/data analyst; UGA B.A. (2015), Georgia State master's in economics (2017); Atlanta DSA leadership. Won the open District 2 seat outright Nov 4, 2025 (64%, 5-candidate field); took office Jan 5, 2026 — first Democratic Socialist on the Atlanta council and its youngest member.

kelsea-bond.notes_set: Atlanta City Council District 2; won the open seat (vacated by Amir Farokhi) outright Nov 4, 2025 with 64% (6,715 votes), no runoff; took office Jan 5, 2026. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement — social-media/platform protocol; minimal council record yet] HARD evidence: self-identified 'abortion rights advocate'; opposes criminalizing abortion care and wants Atlanta to opt out of prosecuting under state law; organized for a Georgia Reproductive Freedom Act (2021) — sanctity_of_life FALSE. Nonbinary (they/them), 'queer organizer in the South'; endorsed by the LGBTQ Victory Fund and LPAC; organized the first labor contingent in the Atlanta Pride Parade (2023); plans city-partnered free name-change clinics for transgender and gender-nonconforming individuals — biblical_marriage FALSE across q0/q2/q4. Core organizer of Atlanta DSA's 'Defund APD, Refund Communities' working group; opposed the Public Safety Training Center; platform backs 'alternatives to policing' — public_justice q0 FALSE. LEAD sponsor of 26-R-3361 (opposing ICE detention facilities in Atlanta, adopted April 2026) and co-sponsor of 26-R-3363 (APD documentation of federal immigration enforcement); called ICE 'terrorizing Latinx and immigrant communities and separating families' — border q2 FALSE. Platform: social housing, mansion/commercial-parking taxes, 'People Over Profits' — noted; the local fiscal-restraint cell left null (no deficit/balanced-budget position). No religion/church stated. NO EVIDENCE on schools/parental rights, Christian liberty, elections (voter ID/paper ballots), bail, or 2A — null, not penalized.

0.note: Self-identified 'abortion rights advocate'; opposes criminalizing abortion care and wants Atlanta to opt out of prosecuting givers or recipients of abortion under state law.

1.note: Organized with abortion-rights groups for a Georgia Reproductive Freedom Act (2021); advocates local non-prosecution of abortion — advocacy contrary to abolition.

0.note: Endorsed by the LGBTQ Victory Fund and LPAC; self-described 'queer organizer in the South' — does not affirm marriage as exclusively one man and one woman.

2.note: Nonbinary, they/them pronouns; plans to partner with local organizations to host free name-change clinics for transgender and gender-nonconforming individuals — contrary to affirming biological sex as immutable.

4.note: Organized the first labor contingent in the Atlanta Pride Parade (2023); Victory Fund/LPAC-backed platform of LGBTQ-affirming city policy — promotion, not opposition.

0.note: Core organizer of Atlanta DSA's 'Defund APD, Refund Communities' working group; opposed the Public Safety Training Center, citing 'corporate interests that advocated for Cop City'; platform backs 'alternatives to policing.'

2.note: Lead sponsor of 26-R-3361 (opposing ICE detention facilities in Atlanta, adopted April 2026) and co-sponsor of 26-R-3363 (APD documentation of federal immigration enforcement); called ICE 'terrorizing Latinx and immigrant communities and separating families.'

profile.religion: Muslim (self-described: 'the pillars of Islam are embedded in my entire upbringing... it has shaped me and my belief in public service')
profile.background: Child of Iranian immigrant parents; former Planned Parenthood Southeast and Partners for Home (homelessness nonprofit) staffer; founded the nonprofit Barefoot Gypsy. Won the Nov 2021 runoff (~66%); District 5 member since Jan 2022; re-elected unopposed Nov 4, 2025 (term to Jan 2030). First nonbinary Atlanta councilmember and first openly queer Muslim elected in Georgia.

liliana-bakhtiari.notes_set: Atlanta City Council District 5; re-elected unopposed Nov 4, 2025, term to Jan 2030. Not on the council for the Sept 2021 training-center lease vote (took office Jan 2022). [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] HARD evidence: LEAD sponsor of post-Dobbs 22-R-3711 (no city funds to record/investigate abortion reports; APD lowest priority) and of 22-R-3981 ($300K of city funds to the National Network of Abortion Funds); official bio credits them with legislation to 'decriminalize abortion following Dobbs' — sanctity_of_life FALSE. First nonbinary councilmember, first openly queer Muslim elected in GA; Victory Fund + Georgia Equality endorsements (Georgia Equality board member); introduced the 2023 resolution pushing back on Georgia's SB 140 ban on gender-transition treatments for trans minors, urging APD lowest-priority enforcement — biblical_marriage FALSE across q0/q2/q4. That same SB 140 resolution is an explicit assertion of local non-enforcement against a state mandate — refuse_state_overreach q0 TRUE (honest credit; in defense of trans procedures, scored FALSE on the marriage cells). One of 4 NO votes on the June 2023 training-center funding (11-4); defended arrested Cop City bail-fund organizers as 'some of the best of our Atlanta mutual aid network,' calling the prosecutions 'weaponizing the law' — public_justice q0 FALSE. Co-sponsor of both April 2026 ICE resolutions (26-R-3361, 26-R-3363) — border q2 FALSE. Called safe-storage mandates 'common sense gun legislation' and said she'd pursue similar in Atlanta (Dec 2021) — storage mandates are not among the q1-enumerated measures (red-flag/mag limits/AWB/registries), so 2A cells left null with this noted. Sponsored the $500K eviction-defense pilot — routine local spending, fiscal cell null. NO EVIDENCE on schools/parental rights, Christian-liberty policy cells, elections, or bail — null, not penalized.

0.note: LEAD sponsor of post-Dobbs 22-R-3711 (no city funds to record/investigate abortion reports; APD lowest priority); official bio credits them with legislation to 'decriminalize abortion following Dobbs.'

1.note: LEAD sponsor of 22-R-3981 donating up to $300,000 of city funds to the National Network of Abortion Funds — active advocacy contrary to abolition.

0.note: First openly queer Muslim elected in Georgia; Victory Fund and Georgia Equality endorsements (Georgia Equality board member); publicly nonmonogamous with two partners — does not affirm marriage as the lifelong union of one man and one woman.

2.note: First nonbinary Atlanta councilmember (they/she); introduced the 2023 resolution pushing back on Georgia's SB 140 ban on gender-transition treatments for trans minors — contrary to affirming biological sex as immutable.

4.note: Sponsored city legislation urging lowest-priority enforcement of the state's trans-minor treatment ban; Georgia Equality board member — promotes LGBTQ+ identity in public policy rather than opposing it.

0.note: One of 4 NO votes on the June 2023 Public Safety Training Center funding (11-4); defended arrested Cop City bail-fund organizers as 'some of the best of our Atlanta mutual aid network,' calling the prosecutions 'weaponizing the law in pursuit of a political agenda.'

Introduced the 2023 resolution asserting city non-enforcement (APD lowest priority) against Georgia SB 140 — an explicit assertion of local control against a state mandate (note: in defense of trans-minor procedures, scored FALSE on the marriage cells).

2.note: Co-sponsor of both April 2026 resolutions: 26-R-3361 (opposing ICE detention facilities in Atlanta) and 26-R-3363 (APD documentation/transparency on federal immigration enforcement; bodycam preservation).
